Patch Set 11:

if we can't update last_rcvd, then we probably should go read-only immediately. otherwise we can't recognize executed-already operations and risk to break filesystem.

Patch Set 11:

> if we can't update last_rcvd, then we probably should go read-only immediately. otherwise we can't recognize executed-already operations and risk to break filesystem.

What about other failures while stopping the transaction? The same?

Patch Set 11:

> What about other failures while stopping the transaction? The same?
I'd say "yes", but I think it makes sense to discuss in a wider audience.

John, I think transaction commit failure in real world conditions does lead to remount-readonly (or panic, depending on your mount options), so we are fine there.

In general I am fine with this patch, though this last test session was a bit catastrophic. I wonder if we should just rebase to try again?

parents.subject: LU-8900 mgs: use reference count for fs_db

commit.subject: LU-8411 ofd: handle last_rcvd file can't update properly
commit.message:
LU-8411 ofd: handle last_rcvd file can't update properly

last_rcvd update may fail but "no fail" return code will
be sent to client. DIO request may be replayed in that case instead
of resend, but as no fail return code send to client, user
application will free a buffer, so replay will be sent with incorrect
data.

Write should fail if last_rcvd can't update properly.

This patch causes sanity test 407 to fail or has brought out an
existing bug in Lustre. sanity test 407 is added to the
ALWAYS_EXCPET list.

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-3609
Signed-off-by: Artem Blagodarenko <artem.blagodarenko@seagate.com>

Change-Id: Idcbff5fd990edbc84539197da9876748b33795dd
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/21398
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
